task: Implemented direction_proposal flow to surface talk-only sessions for user approval before autonomous execution, replacing hard binary skip logic with three-way routing (approve/reject/edit).
discussion_summary: User identified a critical gap: research sessions ending with implementation decisions were being skipped by Signal 3 logic, causing autonomous runs to use stale direction from older coding sessions. Solution: talk-only sessions now return `proposed=True` instead of being discarded, triggering a notification with three buttons (Approve Direction / Reject / Edit Direction) in VS Code. This preserves user intent while maintaining safety — autonomous execution only proceeds if user explicitly approves.

accomplishments.what: Modified lifecycle.py Signal 3 to return proposed=True for talk-only sessions instead of skipping them
Updated stop.py to route direction_proposal notifications with three-button UI when proposed=True
Added direction_proposal handler to extension.js with Approve/Reject/Edit buttons and user intent preservation

next_actions.action: Commit the three modified files (lifecycle.py, stop.py, extension.js) with message 'feat(lifecycle/stop/extension): direction_proposal — talk-only sessions surface for approval'
next_actions.why: Changes are staged but not yet committed; git push will fail without this

next_actions.action: End-to-end test: run a research conversation, reach a conclusion like 'let's implement X', trigger stop hook, verify direction_proposal notification appears in VS Code with three buttons
next_actions.why: Validates the full flow from talk-only session detection through user approval UI

next_actions.action: Test all three button paths: (1) Approve Direction → verify autonomous session starts with proposed direction, (2) Reject → verify no session starts, (3) Edit Direction → verify input box appears and custom direction is saved
next_actions.why: Ensures user has full control over autonomous execution intent

next_actions.action: Verify Signal 3 correctly skips older talk-only sessions and finds the most recent coding session as fallback if user rejects the proposal
next_actions.why: Maintains safety: if user rejects a research conclusion, autonomous run should still have valid direction from last confirmed coding session

next_actions.action: Update implementation_state.md with completion status and commit
next_actions.why: Keeps handover state synchronized with actual progress

decisions.decision: Talk-only sessions return proposed=True instead of being silently skipped
decisions.reason: Preserves user intent from research conclusions while maintaining explicit approval gate for autonomous execution

decisions.decision: Three-button UI (Approve/Reject/Edit) instead of auto-executing on proposal
decisions.reason: User retains full control; prevents autonomous runs from misinterpreting research discussions as implementation directives

decisions.decision: Signal 3 still walks history to find fallback coding session if proposal is rejected
decisions.reason: Safety: ensures autonomous execution always has valid direction, even if user rejects the most recent talk-only session

failed_approaches.approach: Hard binary skip of talk-only sessions in Signal 3
failed_approaches.reason: User identified that research sessions ending with implementation decisions were being discarded, causing autonomous runs to use stale direction from older coding sessions

relational_files.why: Signal 3 logic is the core of direction inference; modified to return proposed=True for talk-only sessions
Stop hook routes the direction_proposal notification; updated to handle three-way user response
VS Code extension displays the direction_proposal UI and handles user button clicks
Tracks session progress and uncommitted changes
